content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Found 2 fix-now correctness issues: nearby-work short aliases can be advertised as valid and then rejected during final-review canonicalization, and the prompt-review nearby-work drift check is inconsistent with the heading syntax the contract validator accepts.

## Amendments

1. `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:467-500` no longer preserves the nearby-work short-alias safety rule from `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:560-573`. The prompt extractor only allowlists a nearby short alias when it belongs to the active milestone, but the canonicalizer rebuilds alias targets from every canonical bead ID whose suffix happens to match an allowlisted short alias. In a prompt that contains both `current-ms.bead-4` and `other-ms.bead-4`, reviewers are told that `bead-4` is valid for the local bead, yet `PlannedElsewhereIdCanonicalizer` marks it ambiguous and downgrades accepted `planned-elsewhere` findings back to `fix-now`. Preserve the same-milestone check when constructing alias targets (or derive the displayed allowlist from the canonicalizer) so foreign nearby beads cannot poison a local short alias.
   Rationale: This breaks the new nearby-work routing path in exactly the collision case the extractor was trying to handle safely, and it can force unnecessary restarts or duplicate work instead of mapping findings to the adjacent bead.
2. `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:382-425` parses `## Nearby work` with an exact `line == "## Nearby work"` match, but the contract validator accepts canonical headings after `trim_end()` in `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:168-176`. A valid contract-marked prompt override that uses `## Nearby work ` (trailing spaces) will pass validation, but the new prompt-review guard will fail to locate the section and stop comparing the nearby-work body. In that state a refiner can rewrite or drop nearby bead IDs without tripping the 'preserve Nearby work verbatim' protection. Normalize heading lines the same way as the validator before extracting section bodies.
   Rationale: The branch explicitly hardened prompt review to preserve graph-derived nearby context, but the implementation does not actually cover all prompt shapes that the contract still considers valid.
